because otherwise they wasted hours and hours and hours and hours getting prepared to buy. The other stuff I gave you, I gave you a whole pile of, this is the precursor to weekly faxes, I gave you a whole pile of my Monday morning memos from the Success Track business from 1988 with Chiropractors and Dennis. These went to mail every Monday morning, mail with a stamp. And there were studying, again there's a lot of different things done throughout here that I'm afraid I'm not going to take that this one for example is nagging. Have you done the following things that we've been telling you to do? Yes or no? There were studying and there's a whole bunch of stuff in them that I'm not going to take the time to call you attention to, but there's a stack of those and then there's a year's supply of the weekly faxes we sent to Gold Plus and above. And if you carefully study both the Monday morning memos and the weekly faxes there's a couple of things that you'll notice. One, I haven't changed much of what I'm doing from 1988 to now in those weekly memos and the component parts are as follows. They are continually challenging them on are they doing what they're supposed to be doing? Have they used the stuff we gave them? There's only four months left in the year, where are you? Continually nagging and challenging them and calling them to task on their behavior. There are pep talks. So there's literally it's one step away from the sales manager calling everybody together in the morning and give them a little 15-20 minute and sending them out to kill the troops. Okay there's some of those. There's recognition of members who are doing right things and getting good results. There are plugs from very subtle to huge and overt. There is some humor. You don't have to be funny to keep people around but it is helpful. And there is topical material. References that connect to what is going on in the news. And so that's sort of the short list that governs what goes into the weeklies. You should very shortly then have the section of consulting documents that follow the coaching documents. It's probably another pink sheet in your notebook to help you find it. I think, maybe not. Yeah it's another pink sheet. So find the pink sheet and you'll be into the consulting documents. And here we have no page numbers. So, lots of luck. Here's a letter that looks like this. Okay this is a letter to a client making a big deal out of squeezing them in for a day that they wanted. So I'm whining for an entire paragraph here about how difficult it is going to be to even squeeze them in and accommodate them and they're ruining my holiday vacation. Et cetera, et cetera, et cetera. Then there's a little conflict paragraph and ultimately I offer them a few dates. Here's, and I don't honestly know how much I'm jumping from page to page. But here's a page that looks like this. What's instructive about this is the second paragraph is all about how I can't take them and shouldn't take them and won't take them and may not be able to take them and all of that. So it's all takeaway selling. Up in the first paragraph though, however, this guy happens to be this person who inquired happens to be in the in the tooth whitening products business. So up in the first paragraph you'll see that I he did insert a sentence that is directly relevant qualifications to him designed to make him want to get to me even more than he did when he inquired in the first place in case he wasn't already aware of that. In the third paragraph I kind of lay out the money. So there's no point in bothering me if you're not at least ready to ante up 50. There's a letter. See there's nothing highlighted on there. I'll skip that.
